methodology.winner_criteria: Primary metric must show ≥10% lift at 95% confidence. Test must run minimum 14 days to account for day-of-week effects. If stat sig not achieved by end date, test is called 'inconclusive' — no winner declared, prior best practice retained.
methodology.notes: For platform-native A/B tests (Meta, Google), use the platform's built-in stat sig calculator. For cross-platform or custom tests, use our internal Stats Calculator in Google Sheets (see testing tool list). Tests must be documented in this file before launch — no undocumented tests allowed.

tools.name: Meta A/B Testing (native)
used_for: Creative A/B tests within Meta campaigns
tools.notes: Preferred for Meta tests. Uses Meta's own stat sig engine. Minimum audience split: 50/50. Cannot run overlapping tests on same audience.

tools.name: Google Ads Campaign Experiments
tools.notes: Set up as Draft > Experiment workflow. Traffic split configured at campaign level. Stat sig shown natively in Experiments tab.

tools.name: Internal Stats Significance Calculator
used_for: Manual stat sig calculation for platforms without native tools
Sample size planning before test launch
tools.notes: Two-proportion z-test. Enter baseline conversion rate, expected lift, and desired confidence level to get required sample size. Owned by Tom Patel.

tools.name: VWO (Visual Website Optimizer)
tools.notes: Used for landing page tests only — not ad creative. Connected to GA4 for conversion tracking. Contact Priya Nair for access.

tests.name: Meta: UGC vs Brand Creative
tests.hypothesis: UGC-style creator content will outperform polished brand creative on Meta prospecting because it feels more native to the feed and drives higher thumb-stop rates, resulting in lower CPA.

variants.name: Brand Creative (Control)
variants.description: Polished brand video with logo, product shots, and branded end card. 30s.

variants.name: UGC Creator Video
variants.description: Authentic-style creator talking-head video filmed on iPhone. No logo until final 3s.

results.conclusion: UGC creative drove 23% lower CPA at 97% confidence. CTR was also 41% higher. Brand creative had higher brand recall in post-view survey but lower direct response performance.
results.action_taken: Scaled UGC approach — now producing 2 new UGC variants per month. Brand creative retained for awareness campaigns only.

tests.name: Google Ads: tROAS vs tCPA Bidding
tests.hypothesis: Switching Search campaigns from tCPA to tROAS bidding will improve revenue efficiency by allowing the algorithm to prioritize higher-value conversions, improving ROAS without significantly increasing CPA.

variants.name: Target CPA (Control)
variants.description: Existing tCPA strategy with CPA target of $8.

variants.name: Target ROAS
variants.description: tROAS strategy set to 500% (5.0x) ROAS target.

results.conclusion: tROAS improved ROAS by 31% (4.5x → 5.9x) with only 8% CPA increase ($5.90 → $6.38). Net revenue impact strongly positive. tROAS drives the algorithm toward higher-value conversion events.
results.action_taken: Switched all Search campaigns to tROAS. CPA campaigns deprecated except for lead gen campaigns where revenue data unavailable.

tests.name: Meta: LAL 1% vs 5% Expansion
tests.hypothesis: 1% LAL audiences (tighter, more similar to seed) will have lower CPA than 5% LAL due to better audience quality, even though reach is smaller.

variants.name: LAL 1% (Control)
variants.description: 1% lookalike of past 180-day purchasers.

variants.name: LAL 5%
variants.description: 5% lookalike of past 180-day purchasers.

results.conclusion: LAL 1% delivered 18% lower CPA. However, LAL 5% delivered 4x more conversions in absolute terms. For volume-constrained campaigns, LAL 5% may be necessary despite efficiency trade-off.
results.action_taken: LAL 1% used as primary for ROAS-optimized campaigns. LAL 5% added as secondary ad set when scaling spend. Broad targeting added as third ad set per CBO setup.

tests.name: Landing Page: Offer vs Feature Headline
tests.hypothesis: A landing page headline focused on the discount offer ('Save 30% Today') will convert at a higher rate than a feature-focused headline ('Built for Modern Homes') by reducing friction for price-sensitive shoppers.

variants.name: Feature Headline (Control)
variants.description: Headline: 'Built for Modern Homes'. Subhead: 'Free shipping on orders over $50.'

variants.name: Offer Headline
variants.description: Headline: 'Save 30% Today Only'. Subhead: 'Plus free shipping on every order.'

results.conclusion: Offer headline drove 22% higher LP conversion rate. Average order value dropped slightly (-4%) as more price-sensitive buyers converted. Net revenue per session increased 17%.
results.action_taken: Offer headline deployed as default. Seasonal offer messaging updated monthly. Feature headline retained for brand-safe placements.

tests.name: LinkedIn: Document Ad vs Single Image
tests.hypothesis: LinkedIn Document Ads (carousel PDFs) will drive higher engagement and lower CPL than single image ads for B2B whitepaper downloads, due to native content preview increasing intent signal.

variants.name: Single Image Ad (Control)
variants.description: Static image ad with whitepaper cover and 'Download Now' CTA.

variants.name: Document Ad
variants.description: LinkedIn Document Ad showing first 3 pages of whitepaper as inline preview before gating.

tests.name: TikTok: Creator Spark Ads vs Brand Video
tests.hypothesis: Spark Ads (boosting organic creator posts) will outperform brand-produced video on TikTok for awareness metrics because they are inherently native and carry social proof.

variants.name: Brand-Produced Video (Control)
variants.description: Brand studio-produced 15s vertical video.

variants.name: Creator Spark Ad
variants.description: Boosting organic post from @creator_handle with 85K followers who organically posted about our product.

tests.notes: Requires creator agreement for Spark Ads authorization. Aisha Williams to coordinate with creator partnerships.

tests.name: DSP Evaluation: DV360 vs. The Trade Desk
tests.hypothesis: The Trade Desk's cross-device graph and UID2.0 identity solution will deliver better prospecting efficiency than DV360 for upper-funnel display, because its open identity infrastructure provides broader cookie-less reach and superior frequency management.

vendor_context.subject: DV360 vs. The Trade Desk
vendor_context.recommendation: Retain DV360 as primary DSP. DV360 delivered 11% lower prospecting CPA and superior integration with CM360 floodlights. The Trade Desk had better frequency management and UID2.0 reach advantages will likely narrow DV360's lead as cookies deprecate — re-evaluate in 12 months.

variants.name: DV360 (Incumbent)
variants.description: DV360 programmatic display using existing Google audience signals, DV360 Optimized Targeting, and CM360 floodlight conversion tracking. $60K spend over 12 weeks.

variants.name: The Trade Desk
variants.description: TTD programmatic display using UID2.0, TTD AI bidding (Koa), and Unified ID 2.0 cross-device reach. Same creative, same targeting parameters where comparable. $60K spend over 12 weeks.

results.conclusion: DV360 delivered 11% lower CPA ($34.20 vs. $38.50). Viewability was comparable (68% vs. 71%). TTD showed 22% better frequency capping cross-device. Brand safety incidents: DV360 0.3%, TTD 0.2%. DV360 reporting depth and floodlight integration rated superior by the team.
results.action_taken: DV360 retained as primary DSP. TTD access maintained for future re-evaluation as cookie deprecation progresses. UID2.0 integration flagged as a 2027 initiative.

tests.name: Agency Evaluation: Incumbent Social Agency vs. Challenger
tests.hypothesis: A boutique social-specialist agency with dedicated creative strategists will outperform the current full-service agency on paid social, because channel specialization drives better creative iteration cycles and faster hypothesis-to-launch timelines.

vendor_context.subject: Full-Service Agency (incumbent) vs. Boutique Social Agency (challenger)
vendor_context.challenger: Social Natives Agency (boutique social specialist, $120K/yr proposal)
evaluation_criteria: Paid social CPA improvement vs. prior quarter
Number of creative variants produced per month
vendor_context.recommendation: Transition to Social Natives Agency. Delivered 18% better CPA, produced 3x more creative variants per month, and introduced a structured test-and-learn process the team lacked. $60K annual savings. Transition plan: 60-day handover starting February 2026.

variants.name: Spark & Co. (Incumbent)
variants.description: Q4 2025 managed by existing full-service agency with dedicated social team of 2 FTE. Campaigns: Meta prospecting, Meta retargeting, LinkedIn B2B.

variants.name: Social Natives Agency (Challenger)
variants.description: Q4 2025 shadowing period: Social Natives provided parallel campaign recommendations, creative strategy, and a 30-day live test on one Meta campaign with $20K budget.

results.conclusion: Social Natives' 30-day live Meta test delivered 18% lower CPA. Creative output: 12 variants vs. 4 from incumbent in same period. Turnaround time: 4 days vs. 11 days. Stat sig not formally achievable (different budgets, different time periods) — decision made on holistic evaluation scorecard.
results.action_taken: Contract awarded to Social Natives Agency. 60-day transition with parallel running began February 2026. Full handover complete March 1, 2026.

tests.name: Ad Network Evaluation: Taboola Native vs. Programmatic Display
tests.hypothesis: Taboola native advertising will reach incremental audiences not accessible via DV360 programmatic display, producing a lower CPM and comparable CPA for upper-funnel awareness content.

vendor_context.subject: Taboola Native Advertising vs. DV360 Programmatic Display
evaluation_criteria: Audience overlap with existing DV360 reach
Minimum spend requirement and buying flexibility
vendor_context.recommendation: Do not add Taboola as a standing channel. CPM was 34% lower but CPA was 61% higher and audience overlap with DV360 was only 12% (confirming incremental reach), but the quality of incremental audience was not sufficient to justify the CPA premium. Re-evaluate if content/editorial strategy changes.

variants.name: DV360 Programmatic Display (Control)
variants.description: DV360 open auction display with contextual and audience targeting. $20K. Same awareness creative used in both arms.

variants.name: Taboola Native
variants.description: Taboola Attentive Reach campaign with interest and contextual targeting on Taboola publisher network. $20K. Same awareness creative adapted to native format (thumbnail + headline).

results.conclusion: Taboola delivered 34% lower CPM ($3.20 vs. $4.85) but 61% higher CPA ($55.20 vs. $34.30). Audience overlap analysis via CM360 showed only 12% overlap — Taboola does reach incremental users but conversion quality was lower. CTR on Taboola 3x higher (0.9% vs. 0.3%) but post-click conversion rate 4x lower.
results.action_taken: Taboola not added to standing media plan. Learnings documented for future content-led campaign consideration.

tests.name: MMM Vendor Evaluation: Northstar vs. Analytic Partners
tests.hypothesis: Analytic Partners' on-demand MMM platform will provide more actionable budget optimization recommendations with faster turnaround than our current quarterly Northstar MMM, enabling more responsive budget reallocation across channels.

vendor_context.subject: Northstar MMM (incumbent) vs. Analytic Partners ROI Genome (challenger)
evaluation_criteria: Turnaround time for budget scenario analysis
Self-serve capability for the media team

variants.name: Northstar MMM (Incumbent)
variants.description: Quarterly model runs with 6-week delivery. Covers all paid channels. Budget scenario modeling available via analyst request. Current contract runs through December 2026.

variants.name: Analytic Partners ROI Genome
variants.description: Continuous model with weekly data refresh. Self-serve scenario planning UI. Pilot access granted April-June 2026 on 6-month evaluation.
